description: Analyze a primed lexical decision dataset at ~/pebl/data/lexdec_priming_data.csv. The dataset contains 120 trials per participant across 4 conditions (related_word, unrelated_word, related_nonword, unrelated_nonword). Compute semantic priming effects (unrelated-word RT - related-word RT for correct trials), lexicality effects (correct nonword RT - correct word RT), and d-prime (Z(hit rate) - Z(false alarm rate)) per participant. Hit is defined as responding 'word' to a word target, and False Alarm (FA) as responding 'word' to a nonword target. Use standard corrections for perfect scores (replace 0 with 0.5/N and 1 with (N-0.5)/N). Exclude participant sub-99 from group analysis. Produce a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/priming_report.json containing 'participants' (with id, priming_effect_ms, lexicality_effect_ms, d_prime, or excluded: true) and 'group_means' for valid participants.

metadata.contamination_description: chance accuracy (~50%), no priming effect (~0ms), no lexicality effect (~0ms), d' near 0
